name: sox
description: SOX 404 rules for code paths affecting financial reporting integrity — audit trail, segregation of duties, change management, data integrity, access control. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition.

severity_rules.pattern: Database write or update on a financial-records table (revenue, ledger, journal, accruals, GL) without a corresponding audit-log row capturing actor, before-value, after-value, and timestamp.
severity_rules.description: SOX 404 internal controls require a tamper-evident audit trail for every mutation of financial data.
severity_rules.remediation: Emit an immutable audit-log row (or trigger-based shadow table) on every financial-records mutation.

severity_rules.pattern: Same actor (user, service account, role) can both initiate and approve a financial transaction in the same code path or workflow.
severity_rules.description: Segregation of duties is a core SOX control — initiation and approval must be performed by different actors.
severity_rules.remediation: Split the workflow into initiate/approve steps with role checks that exclude the initiator from the approver pool.

severity_rules.pattern: Production deployment of code touching financial-reporting modules without a referenced change ticket or PR approval from the financial-controls reviewer group.
severity_rules.description: SOX requires documented change management with approval evidence for any change to financial systems.
severity_rules.remediation: Require a change-ticket reference and reviewer approval at the merge gate for financial modules.

severity_rules.pattern: Financial calculation (sum, total, tax, FX conversion) without bounds validation, rounding policy declaration, or integrity checksum.
severity_rules.description: Material misstatements in financial calculations breach SOX 302 (CEO/CFO certification of accuracy).
severity_rules.remediation: Use a fixed-point/decimal type with declared rounding mode; validate inputs against bounds; emit a checksum on aggregated totals.

severity_rules.pattern: Financial reporting endpoint or admin tool grants access to a broad role (e.g., `staff: *`) instead of a narrowly-scoped finance role.
severity_rules.description: SOX requires least-privilege access on financial systems with documented role-to-function mapping.
severity_rules.remediation: Define a finance-specific role; require approval and quarterly review on grants; remove generic broad-role access.

severity_rules.pattern: Financial event with materiality threshold reached (e.g., revenue recognition, write-off) without a disclosure-trigger hook or notification path.
severity_rules.description: SOX 409 requires real-time disclosure of material financial events; the code path needs a hook into the disclosure workflow.
severity_rules.remediation: Wire material-event detection into the disclosure workflow; document the materiality thresholds in code.
